- `EntryOpen` — an entry has been opened via `putNextEntry()` and data can be written
- `Finished` — `finish()` was called, ZIP is finalized, underlying stream still open
- `Closed` — terminal state; stream is fully closed

- `write()` is only valid in `EntryOpen` (there must be an active entry to receive data)
- `closeEntry()` is only valid in `EntryOpen`
- `putNextEntry()` in `EntryOpen` implicitly closes the current entry (self-loop semantics)
